metadata.title: Barrier Transmission Explorer — Exact vs WKB

beats.narration_text: Hei. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of barrier transmission explorer. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of barrier transmission explorer
output: ✓ Barrier Transmission Explorer — Exact vs WKB

beats.narration_text: Barrier Transmission Explorer is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Exact vs WKB — resonances above barrier hit T=1 exactly like an anti-reflection coating. Each Å below barrier changes T by ×7.4.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Barrier Transmission Explorer. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Barrier V₀ (eV), Width L (nm), Energy E (eV) [cursor]. Verify: Exact vs WKB — resonances above barrier hit T=1 exactly like an anti-reflection coating. Each Å below barrier changes T . Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Barrier Transmission Explorer. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Barrier V₀ (eV), Width L (nm), Energy E (

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from vol1-barrier-explorer.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: barrier transmission explorer. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag Barrier V₀ (eV) from 0.5 to 6. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of Barrier V₀ (eV) from 0.5 to 6

beats.narration_text: Exact vs WKB — resonances above barrier hit T=1 exactly like an anti-reflection coating. Each Å below barrier changes T by ×7.4.. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of barrier transmission explorer. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Barrier Transmission Explorer simulation and add a second slider that controls Barrier V₀ (eV). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
